title: Systemic PRNG call-count phase desync (rng_seed) — residual combat divergence after sim-tick alignment: guards perceive/act a few ticks off retail
repro: dam_combat_guard6 both-sides under --align tick (FID-0062): after removing the alignment artifact, residual guard divergence (315 actiontype) is driven by native vs retail PRNG randomGetNext() being called a different NUMBER of times per tick (call-count phase), so probabilistic see-target (randomGetNext()%distance==0) fires a few ticks off — e.g. guard6 perceives +108t native vs +76t stock. The PRNG itself is bit-faithful (random.c:279); the CALL COUNT diverges.

history.note: Filed from FID-0062 re-validation. THE real systemic owner of the residual perception-timing divergence that survives correct sim-tick alignment. NOT a guard-AI bug (perception logic is faithful ASM). FID-0054 = mostly artifact + THIS. FID-0011/0012/0013 must be re-captured under --align tick; whatever survives is THIS class (RNG call-count phase + tiny position drift), not chr.c/stan.c logic. Root-cause needs: find where native makes a different number of randomGetNext() calls per tick than retail (extra/missing consumers, order, or a call gated differently). XL systemic. Related: rng_seed §5/§8, FID-0046 (Streets nondeterminism).

Round-1 measurement: per-tick call-count differential infrastructure landed (tools/fidelity/rng_callcount_diff.py, ctest rng_callcount_diff_unittest; seeds->counts via 33-bit PRNG chain walk, validated 218/218 ticks against the exact native rng.call_count counter). Natural route: rate parity pre-combat within schedule noise; fire burst on the same tick both sides (282v287); post-engagement bursts phase-shifted (the +76/+108t perception offset); NOT a gross constant-rate class. Seed-locked route (dam_combat_guard6_rngseedlock, both sides g_randomSeed=0x1D8F3CC2B at gameplay frame 40): SAME seed in one idle frame -> retail draws 15, native 13; systemic idle-weighted bias stock +2.1 draws/frame (cum -15 -> -99 over 40 idle frames), flattening to +0.5/frame walking; streams never re-sync (seed_match_ticks 0). Native side of the locked frame fully attributed (shuffle x3, idle-roll x4, sight x2, ai x1, tickstand x1, viewmodel x2). Ruled out: idle-roll cadence (breathing=0 both sides, bondhead.c:887), port viewmodel prep (ASM-anchored: 7F060FF0 scale / 7F06105C roll, same guards). Remaining: guard-AI pool/cadence vs Bond-frame consumer -> needs retail callsite hook (round-2 map in derivation). BONUS FIX: ares combat.rng_seed read the BE HIGH word (0/1 after first step) -> part of the sec5/sec8 per-frame rng_seed divergence was instrumentation artifact; patched to base+4.

Round 2: retail caller-PC attribution via the new ares randomGetNext PC hook (MGB64_ARES_RNG_PC_TRACE, cpu.cpp dispatch compare at VRAM 0x7000A450; 39904 retail calls logged, seed-chain-validated). Consumer sets are 1:1 (derivation sec9.2 census: shuffle 3/f both, muzzle pair 2/f both, bheadUpdateIdleRoll 4-draw group cadence-matched, chrlvTickStand 4.35 vs 4.38/f, ai/AI_SetNewRandom ~equal). The ENTIRE residual (+1.2-1.5/f idle) is the geometry-conditional perception draw chrCheckTargetInSight (retail 0x7F029FE0 = chrlv.c:5749, 3.08 vs 1.60/f): identical consumer at the identical program point on both sides; the RATE differs because patrolling guards (chrnum 2,39-45) sit 300-2000u apart at aligned ticks (27+ static guards byte-identical) — patrol-phase state divergence from the pre-route history (menu vs direct boot, natural seeds), upstream of the RNG layer. First-frame ground truth corrects round 1: retail 12 vs native 13 (the '15' was RDRAM dcache-writeback smear). No missing/extra/reordered consumer exists; charter 4.2 fix N/A. D5 closure gated on FID-0054/0055 guard-state parity. Headline machine-reproducible: rng_callcount_diff.py --lock-seed (lock_frame block; unit-tested).

FID-0014 (patrol magic semantics) landed and did NOT move the native RNG schedule on dam_combat_guard6_agealign: native rng.call_count stream bit-identical pre/post fix (0/681 records differ); rng_callcount_diff vs the pinned stock capture unchanged (cum -5979 over 114 common ticks). The pre-onset patrollers sit outside chrCheckTargetInSight's range/cone pre-checks on this route, so their position divergence never reached the probabilistic draw here. The Sec.9.2 perception-class attribution needs re-measurement on a route where patrolling guards approach the player before D5 sequencing can lean on FID-0014.
